source: CIS GOOGLE CLOUD PLATFORM FOUNDATION BENCHMARK V4.0.0
title: Ensure That There Are Only GCP-Managed Service Account Keys for Each Service Account

input_row.source: CIS GOOGLE CLOUD PLATFORM FOUNDATION BENCHMARK V4.0.0
input_row.title: Ensure That There Are Only GCP-Managed Service Account Keys for Each Service Account
input_row.description: User-managed service accounts should not have user-managed keys.
input_row.rationale: Anyone who has access to the keys will be able to access resources through the service account. GCP-managed keys are used by Cloud Platform services such as App Engine and Compute Engine. These keys cannot be downloaded. Google will keep the keys and automatically rotate them on an approximately weekly basis. User-managed keys are created, downloadable, and managed by users. They expire 10 years from creation. For user-managed keys, the user has to take ownership of key management activities which include: • Key storage • Key distribution • Key revocation • Key rotation • Protecting the keys from unauthorized users • Key recovery Even with key owner precautions, keys can be easily leaked by common development malpractices like checking keys into the source code or leaving them in the Downloads directory, or accidentally leaving them on support blogs/channels. It is recommended to prevent user-managed service account keys. Impact: Deleting user-managed service account keys may break communication with the applications using the corresponding keys.
input_row.audit: From Google Cloud Console 1. Go to the IAM page in the GCP Console using https://console.cloud.google.com/iam-admin/iam 2. In the left navigation pane, click Service accounts. All service accounts and their corresponding keys are listed. 3. Click the service accounts and check if keys exist. From Google Cloud CLI List All the service accounts: gcloud iam service-accounts list Identify user-managed service accounts which have an account EMAIL ending with iam.gserviceaccount.com For each user-managed service account, list the keys managed by the user: gcloud iam service-accounts keys list --iam-account=<Service Account> -- managed-by=user No keys should be listed.
input_row.remediation: From Google Cloud Console 1. Go to the IAM page in the GCP Console using https://console.cloud.google.com/iam-admin/iam 2. In the left navigation pane, click Service accounts. All service accounts and their corresponding keys are listed. 3. Click the service account. 4. Click the edit and delete the keys. From Google Cloud CLI To delete a user managed Service Account Key, gcloud iam service-accounts keys delete --iam-account=<user-managed-service- account-EMAIL> <KEY-ID> Prevention: You can disable service account key creation through the Disable service account key creation Organization policy by visiting https://console.cloud.google.com/iam- admin/orgpolicies/iam-disableServiceAccountKeyCreation. Learn more at: https://cloud.google.com/resource-manager/docs/organization-policy/restricting-service- accounts In addition, if you do not need to have service accounts in your project, you can also prevent the creation of service accounts through the Disable service account creation Organization policy: https://console.cloud.google.com/iam- admin/orgpolicies/iam-disableServiceAccountCreation. Default Value: By default, there are no user-managed keys created for user-managed service accounts. References: 1. https://cloud.google.com/iam/docs/understanding-service- accounts#managing_service_account_keys 2. https://cloud.google.com/resource-manager/docs/organization-policy/restricting- service-accounts Additional Information: A user-managed key cannot be created on GCP-Managed Service Accounts.
input_row.impact: Deleting user-managed service account keys may break communication with the applications using the corresponding keys.
input_row.references: 1. https://cloud.google.com/iam/docs/understanding-service- accounts#managing_service_account_keys 2. https://cloud.google.com/resource-manager/docs/organization-policy/restricting- service-accounts Additional Information: A user-managed key cannot be created on GCP-Managed Service Accounts.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:**

- ✓ Can GCP APIs query this property? → Automated
- ✓ Is this a standard GCP service property (encryption, logging, access control, network rules)? → Automated
- ✓ Does Google Cloud SDK (gcloud) SDK have methods to check this? → Automated
- ✓ Does GCP CLI have commands to retrieve this? → Automated
- ✗ Does it require SSH/RDP to instances? → Manual
- ✓ Does audit mention Google Cloud SDK (gcloud)/GCP CLI commands? → Automated
- ✓ Is it about IAM, S3, EC2, VPC, CloudTrail, Config, KMS, etc.? → Automated (GCP APIs expose these)
- ✗ Is it about application code, OS configs, or visual review? → Manual

This control is about ensuring that there are only GCP-managed service account keys for each service account. GCP provides API and CLI methods to list and manage service account keys, specifically separating user-managed keys from system-managed keys. The listed `gcloud` command allows to enumerate and verify if user-managed keys are present, fitting well within an automated audit framework.

**PROGRAM NAME:** gcp_iam_service_account_no_user_managed_keys

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**

- **GCP API Calls/Gcloud SDK Methods to Use:**
  - Use `gcloud iam service-accounts list` to enumerate all service accounts.
  - For each service account, execute `gcloud iam service-accounts keys list --iam-account=<Service Account> --managed-by=user` to check for user-managed keys.

- **GCP CLI Commands (if applicable):**
  - `gcloud iam service-accounts list`
  - `gcloud iam service-accounts keys list --iam-account=<Service Account> --managed-by=user`

- **Specific Resources to Enumerate:**
  - Enumerate all service accounts within a GCP project or organization.

- **Exact Configuration Properties/Settings to Validate:**
  - Ensure no user-managed keys are listed for any service account.

- **Clear Pass/Fail Criteria with Expected Values:**
  - **Pass:** No user-managed keys are found for any service account.
  - **Fail:** One or more user-managed keys are found for any service account. 

This audit provides a clear and actionable method to ensure compliance with the CIS benchmark by leveraging Google Cloud's native IAM capabilities and commands.
step2_review:
---

### 1. **APPROACH VALIDATION:** AGREE

- **Manual vs Automated Classification:**  
  The classification as "Automated" is correct. GCP provides full API and CLI support for enumerating service accounts and their keys. No manual review or SSH/RDP access is required.
- **GCP API/SDK/CLI Support:**  
  - The `gcloud iam service-accounts list` command enumerates all service accounts.
  - The `gcloud iam service-accounts keys list --iam-account=<service-account> --managed-by=user` command lists only user-managed keys.
  - These operations are fully supported by GCP APIs and can be scripted for automation.
- **Other GCP Automation Options:**  
  - GCP Cloud Asset Inventory and the IAM API (`projects.serviceAccounts.keys.list`) can also be used for automation.
  - GCP Config (now called "Config Controller") does not natively monitor IAM keys, so the CLI/API approach is preferred.

---

### 2. **TECHNICAL ACCURACY:** 9/10

- **Automation Details:**  
  - The listed commands are accurate and sufficient for the control.
  - The use of `--managed-by=user` is correct and filters out system-managed keys.
- **Program Name Convention:**  
  - The name `gcp_iam_service_account_no_user_managed_keys` is descriptive, but could be slightly improved for clarity (see below).
- **Completeness:**  
  - The steps are complete for a project-level audit.
  - For organization-wide audits, the process should clarify how to enumerate service accounts across all projects (e.g., using `gcloud projects list` and iterating).
- **Pass/Fail Criteria:**  
  - Clear and actionable.

---

### 3. **IMPROVEMENTS:**

- **Organization-wide Coverage:**  
  - The current approach assumes a single project. For enterprise environments, recommend iterating over all projects in an organization:
    ```sh
    for PROJECT in $(gcloud projects list --format="value(projectId)"); do
      for SA in $(gcloud iam service-accounts list --project="$PROJECT" --format="value(email)"); do
        gcloud iam service-accounts keys list --iam-account="$SA" --managed-by=user --project="$PROJECT"
      done
    done
    ```
- **Alternative API Methods:**  
  - The REST API `projects.serviceAccounts.keys.list` can be used for direct integration or custom automation.
  - Cloud Asset Inventory (CAI) can export IAM key resources for large-scale audits.
- **Program Name Suggestion:**  
  - Consider: `gcp_iam_service_account_no_user_keys` or `gcp_iam_service_account_user_key_absence` for brevity and clarity.
- **Explicitly State Scope:**  
  - Clarify whether the audit is per project or organization-wide.
- **Edge Cases:**  
  - Mention that some service accounts may be in deleted projects or folders; clarify handling.

---

### 4. **CONFIDENCE:** HIGH

- The assessment is technically sound and aligns with GCP security best practices.
- The commands and logic are valid and widely used for this control.

---

### 5. **FINAL RECOMMENDATION:** MODIFY

- **Keep the automated classification.**
- **Modify** the documentation to:
  - Explicitly mention how to scale the audit organization-wide.
  - Suggest alternative API methods for large environments.
  - Slightly improve the program name for clarity.
- **Sample improved summary:**
  > "This control can be fully automated using GCP IAM APIs or gcloud CLI. For organization-wide coverage, enumerate all projects and all service accounts within each project. Use `gcloud iam service-accounts keys list --managed-by=user` or the IAM API to ensure no user-managed keys exist. Program name suggestion: `gcp_iam_service_account_no_user_keys`."

---

**Summary Table:**

| Section                | Assessment         | Notes                                                                                 |
|------------------------|-------------------|---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| Approach Validation    | AGREE             | Fully automatable via GCP API/CLI                                                     |
| Technical Accuracy     | 9/10              | Accurate, minor improvements for org-wide coverage and naming                         |
| Improvements           | See above         | Org-wide audit, alternative APIs, clarify program name                                |
| Confidence             | HIGH              | Standard GCP security control, well-supported                                         |
| Final Recommendation   | MODIFY            | Add org-wide audit details, suggest improved program name, keep as automated          |

---
